instructions: 1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. 2. Lower into a partial squat, keeping back straight. 3. Explosively jump up, twisting your body 180 degrees in mid-air. 4. Start in a standard push-up position. As you lower your body, lift your right foot off the floor and swing your right knee out sideways towards your right elbow. Push back up to the starting position while returning your leg. Repeat on the left side, bringing your left knee towards your left elbow.

Start in a split squat stance with one leg forward and one back. Lower into a squat then explosively jump up, switching your legs in mid-air so you land with the opposite leg forward. Immediately lower into the next squat.
Simulate the rotational movements of swinging a bat or throwing a ball. Focus on generating power from the hips and transferring it through the torso. Include practice swings and sprints.
Practice foot dexterity and coordination by keeping a footbag (hacky sack) off the ground using your feet, knees, and chest. Perform stalls and passes.
Engage in soccer-specific drills: dribbling cones, passing against a wall, shooting practice, and short sprints. Focus on agility and ball control.
Practice lateral shuffles, split steps, and swing mechanics. Focus on quick bursts of movement and reaction time drills.
Run at maximum speed for a short distance or time interval. Recover fully or actively before the next sprint. Maintain upright posture and drive your knees high.
Start in a squat position. Jump your feet out wide while staying low in the squat (like a jumping jack). Jump feet back together. Keep the chest up throughout.

Exercise name: Squats to Side Leg Raises  Lifts
instructions: Perform a standard bodyweight squat. As you stand up, lift one leg out to the side (abduction). Lower the foot and immediately go into the next squat, alternating sides.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Ab Pike Press
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your shins on a stability ball. Keep your legs straight. Lift your hips high towards the ceiling, pulling the ball in towards your hands (pike). Lower back to flat plank.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Back Extensions
instructions: Lie face down with your stomach on the stability ball and feet braced against a wall or heavy object. Place hands behind your head. Lower your chest over the ball, then fast lifting your torso using your lower back muscles.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Bench Russian Twists
instructions: Lie with your upper back supported on the stability ball, feet flat on the floor, hips lifted (bridge position). Clasp hands together and rotate your torso to one side, rolling onto one shoulder, then rotate to the other side.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Bicep Curls
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with feet flat on the floor. Hold dumbbells. Perform bicep curls while maintaining balance and an upright posture.
Lie with your lower back on the stability ball, feet flat on the floor. Crunch your upper body forward, contracting your abs. Lower back down until your back slightly arches over the ball.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Dumbbell Chest Flyes
instructions: Lie with your upper back on the stability ball, hips engaged in a bridge. Hold dumbbells above your chest. Lower them out to the sides in a wide arc, then bring them back together performing a flye.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions: Lie with your upper back on the stability ball, hips elevated in a bridge. Press dumbbells from chest level straight up towards the ceiling.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit upright on a stability ball. Press dumbbells from shoulder height overhead until arms are extended.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Hamstring Leg Curl  Hip Raise  Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with heels on the stability ball. Lift hips into a bridge. Curl the ball towards your glutes by bending your knees, then extend legs back out.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Hand Off  V-Pass
instructions: Lie on your back holding the ball between your hands. Lift legs and arms. Pass the ball from hands to feet. Lower limbs towards floor (without touching). Lift back up and pass ball from feet to hands.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Knee Tuck to Chest
instructions: Start in a push-up position with shins on the ball. Tuck your knees in towards your chest, rolling the ball forward. Extend legs back out to plank.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Leg Lifts
instructions: Lie face down over the ball with hands on the floor for balance. Lift legs straight up behind you using glutes and lower back.
Place your forearms on the stability ball and extend legs back into a plank position. Hold the position, keeping your body in a straight line.

instructions: Perform push-ups with your hands on top of the stability ball to increase instability and core recruitment.

Exercise name: Stability  Swiss  Exercise Ball Reverse Crunches

Stand holding a dumbbell in one hand. Lean to that side, then use your obliques to pull back to center and slightly past.
Stand holding a dumbbell with both hands. Rotate torso/arms side to side.
Stand holding dumbbells at sides. Curl them up towards shoulders, rotating palms up.
Stand holding dumbbells with neutral grip (palms facing body). Curl up keeping palms facing each other.
Stand holding dumbbells at sides. Raise heels off the floor.
Stand holding dumbbells at shoulder height. Press straight up overhead.
Stand with feet hip-width. Hinge at hips pushing them back, keeping slight bend in knees, until torso is nearly parallel to floor. Squeeze glutes to stand.

Exercise name: Standing Front Shoulder Plate  Dumbbell  Kettlebell Raises
instructions: Stand holding a weight (plate, DB, KB) with both hands. Raise it straight in front to shoulder height.
Stand and reach down towards your toes (or prop one leg up on a bench) to stretch the hamstrings.
Stand tall. Lift one knee towards your chest and grab it with both hands. Pull it gently closer to hug it. Release and switch legs.
Stand with feet hip-width apart. Shift weight to one leg. Curl the other heel towards your glutes using your hamstring. Lower slowly.
Stand with feet shoulder-width. Swing arms back and bend knees. Explosively jump forward as far as possible. Land softly with knees bent.
Stand holding a barbell overhead with arms extended. Lower the bar behind your head by bending only at the elbows. Press back up to start.

Exercise name: Standing Overhead Military Barbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Stand with barbell at shoulder height (front rack). Press the bar straight overhead until arms are locked out. Lower back to collarbone.
Extend arm forward, palm facing down. Use the other hand to gently pull fingers down and back towards you.
Extend arm forward, palm facing up. Use the other hand to gently pull fingers down and back.
Stand on one leg. Grab the foot of the other leg behind you. Pull heel towards glutes.

Exercise name: Standing Resistance Band Chest Pulls  Chest Openers  Back Flyes
instructions: Hold resistance band with both hands in front of chest. Pull hands apart until band touches chest (Reverse Flye).
Stand relaxed. Shake out arms and legs. Sip water. Regulate breathing.
Stand holding barbell with overhand grip (palms down). Curl bar towards chest.
Stand tall. Roll shoulders up, back, and down in a circular motion. Repeat in reverse.
Stand with feet wide. Reach one arm overhead and lean to the opposite side. Feel stretch in obliques/lats.
Stand tall. Lift one leg directly out to the side while keeping toes pointing forward.

Exercise name: Standing Single  One Leg Chair Calf Raises
instructions: Stand on one leg (can hold chair for balance). Raise heel off floor as high as possible. Lower securely.
Stand on one leg. Lift the other slightly and draw circles with the foot/leg.
Stand on a block/plate in the Smith Machine. Bar on shoulders. Lower heels, then raise up on toes.

Exercise name: Standing Supported Resistance Band Hamstring Curls
instructions: Stand facing anchor point. Band around ankle. Curl heel to glute against resistance.

Exercise name: Standing Two-Armed Bent Over Dumbbell Rows
instructions: Stand holding DBs. Hinge forward at hips. Pull both DBs to waist while squeezing back.
Stand holding water bottles. Curl them up to shoulders.
Start in side plank. Lift top leg and raise top arm to create a star shape.
Hold dumbbells with elbows bent at 90 degrees. Maintain this position effectively.
Perform a pushup but hold the bottom position (chest just off floor).
Lower into a squat position. Hold at the bottom (thighs parallel).
Step up onto a box/bench. As you stand, drive the opposite knee up towards chest. Step down.
Stand with barbell. Hinge at hips keeping legs straight (but not locked). Lower bar until hamstring stretch. Pull back up.

Exercise name: Straight Arm Pulldowns  Pull Downs  Pullovers
instructions: Stand facing cable machine, straight bar high pulley. Keep arms straight. Push bar down to thighs using lats.
Stand facing wall. Step one foot back, keep leg straight and heel down. Lean forward.
Hold one DB at chest or hanging. Wide stance, toes out. Squat down.
Wide stance, toes out. Grip bar inside knees (arms hang straight). Hinge/Squat to pull bar.
Lie on stomach. Lift arms and legs simultaneously off floor. Hold.

Exercise name: Supine Lying Down Position  Corpse Pose
instructions: Lie flat on back, arms at sides, palms up. Legs slightly apart. Relax completely.
Kneel on floor. Hands behind head. Step up with right foot, then left to stand. Kneel back down right, then left.
Stand or sit holding Swiss ball. Squeeze it hard with arms or knees.
Face TRX anchor. Lean back extending arms. Curl body up towards handles.

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Strap Hamstring  Leg Curls
instructions: Lie on back, heels in TRX cradles. Lift hips. Curl heels to glutes.
Plank position feet in TRX cradles. Bring alternating knees to chest.

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Strap Pistol  Single  One Leg Squats
instructions: Hold TRX handles. Balance on one leg. Squat down, extending other leg forward. Use arms to assist.
Feet in TRX cradles. Forearms on floor. Hold plank.
Sit under TRX, grabbing handles. Pull chest to handles (Bodyweight row/Pullup variation).

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Strap Reverse Lunges  Lunges
instructions: One foot in TRX cradle behind you. Lunge down with standing leg.
Face TRX anchor. Lean back. Pull chest to handles.
Place your feet in the TRX cradles and support your body on one forearm. Lift your hips off the floor to form a straight line and hold this side plank position.

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Strap Standing Ab Rollout
instructions: Stand facing away from anchor. Hold handles. Lean forward extending arms overhead (Superman). Pull back.
Face anchor. Lean back. Open arms to 'T' shape. Pull back together.

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Straps Atomic Push-ups  Pushups
instructions: Feet in TRX. Pushup position. Perform pushup, then tuck knees to chest.
Face away from anchor. hold handles. Lean forward. Perform fluid chest press.

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Straps Deltoid Y Flyes
instructions: Face anchor. Arms straight overhead in 'Y'. Lean back slightly. Pull body up maintaining Y shape.
Lie on your back with your heels secured in the TRX cradles. Engage your glutes to lift your hips off the floor into a bridge position, maintaining a straight line.
Face anchor. Hold handles overhead. Squat down keeping arms overhead.
Plank position, feet in TRX. Saw body back and forth, then pike hips up.

Exercise name: TRX Suspension Straps Side Step  Lateral Lunges
instructions: Hold TRX. Stand sideways. Step out into lateral lunge.
Hold TRX handles. Lean back slightly. Squat down and up.
Back foot in TRX cradle. Lunge down. (Same as Bulgarian Split Squat in TRX)
Face away from anchor. Arms extended overhead. Bend elbows to lower body/hands behind head.
Lie on back. Legs straight up. Crunch up to touch toes.
Lie on back, knees bent 90 degrees (Tabletop). Tap one toe to floor, then other.

Exercise name: Tricep Cable Rope Push Pull Downs
instructions: High pulley rope. Pull down spreading rope at bottom.
Bend forward at the waist while holding a dumbbell, keeping your elbow high and pinned to your side. Extend your arm back until it is straight, squeezing the tricep.
Parallel bars or bench. Lower body bending elbows. Press up.
Perform an explosive jump straight up into the air. At the peak of the jump, quickly tuck your knees up towards your chest before landing softly.
Lie with KB held up. Roll to elbow, then hand. Lift hips (bridge). Slide leg through. Lunge up to stand. Reverse.
Hold a kettlebell with both hands. Lower into a squat, then as you stand up explosively, swing the kettlebell upwards using the momentum from your hips.
Grip the bar with your palms facing towards you (underhand grip). Pull your body up until your chin is essentially over the bar, then lower yourself with control.
Bench press with reverse grip (palms facing head).
Sit comfortably at the Upper Body Ergometer (UBE). Grip the handles and pedal using your arms in a cyclical motion, keeping your core engaged.
Stand tall holding a barbell with an overhand grip. Pull the bar vertically up towards your chin, leading with your elbows high.
Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs. Pull them vertically straight up towards your chest or chin, focusing on raising your elbows high.
Stand holding a kettlebell handle with both hands. Pull it vertically up towards your chin, keeping your elbows high and the weight close to your body.
Set a cable pulley to the lowest position. Grip the handle and pull it diagonally upwards and across your body, pivoting your back foot.

Exercise name: V-Bar  Triangle Bar Lat Pulldowns  Pull Downs  Pullovers
instructions: V-Bar high pulley. Lean back slightly. Pull to chest.
Walk forward kicking legs high to opposite hand (Frankenstein).
Stand against wall. Arms in 'W' shape. Slide arms up wall.
Lean your back flat against a sturdy wall and walk your feet out. Lower your hips until you are in a half-squat position and hold.

Exercise name: Wall Push-Ups  Pushups  Standing Press Ups
instructions: Stand facing a wall at arm's length. Place your hands on the wall and perform a push-up movement, lowering your chest to the wall and pushing back.
Sit with your back flat against a wall and knees bent at 90 degrees. Hold this position with your thighs parallel to the floor for the desired time.
Lie flat on the floor with holding water bottles in each hand. Press them straight up towards the ceiling like a bench press, then lower them back down.
Stand holding water bottles in your hands for resistance. Step forward into a lunge, lowering your back knee towards the ground, then push back.
Sit or stand with good posture, holding water bottles at shoulder height. Press them straight up overhead until your arms are fully extended.

Exercise name: Water Bottle Single  One Arm Tricep KickbacksKick Backs
instructions: Hinge forward at your hips holding a water bottle with your elbow high. Extend your arm backwards to straighten it, engaging your tricep.

Exercise name: Water Bottle Squat Clean and Presses
instructions: Clean the water bottles to shoulder height. Perform a squat, and as you rise, press the bottles directly overhead in one fluid motion.
Start in a squat position holding water bottles at your shoulders. drive up explosively from the squat and use the momentum to press the bottles overhead.
Hold water bottles at your sides or at chest level for weight. Lower your hips back and down into a squat, then drive through your heels to stand.
Position yourself for a bench dip with a weight plate resting on your lap. Lower your hips by bending your elbows, then push back up to the start.
Hold a weight at your chest (Goblet style). Step out wide and squat deeply to one side, keeping the other leg straight, then return to center.
Lie on your back with a barbell or dumbbell resting comfortably on your hips. Drive your hips up towards the ceiling, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Attach weight via a dip belt or weighted vest. Grip the pull-up bar and pull your chin over the bar, overcoming the added resistance.
Sit on the floor holding a weight, leaning your torso back slightly. Rotate your torso to touch the weight to the floor on each side alternately.
Wear a weighted vest or dip belt. Support yourself on parallel bars and lower your body by bending your elbows, then press back up.

Exercise name: Wide Arm Chest Stretch  Reverse Butterfly Stretch
instructions: Sit or stand tall. Extend your arms out wide to the sides with thumbs pointing back to stretch across your chest.
Place your hands on the floor significantly wider than shoulder-width. Lower your chest towards the floor and push back up, emphasizing the chest muscles.
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width and toes pointed out. Lower into a squat with a barbell on your back, targeting the inner thighs.

Exercise name: Wide-Grip Lat Pulldowns  Pull Downs  Pullovers
instructions: Grip the lat pulldown bar with a wide grip. Pull the bar down towards your upper chest, focusing on driving your elbows down and back.
Lie on your back with your legs lifted straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your shoulders down as you rotate your legs side to side.
Extend your arms and rotate your wrists in circular motions, first clockwise and then counter-clockwise, to improve mobility.
